articles.title: Launch Family Entertainment Reports Strategic Growth in First Half of 2026, Including New Smaller-Format Park Model
articles.summary: Launch Family Entertainment continued expanding its indoor FEC franchise in H1 2026, highlighted by the debut of a smaller-format park model designed to give franchisees more real estate flexibility. This development is significant for prospective FEC franchisees who may have been priced out of larger footprint requirements. The smaller format could accelerate franchise growth in secondary and tertiary markets.

articles.title: $50M EVO Entertainment Complex Greenlit for Downtown Clearwater
articles.summary: A $50 million EVO Entertainment complex has been cleared for development in downtown Clearwater, Florida, signaling continued large-scale investment in multi-concept FEC venues. EVO Entertainment is known for combining bowling, gaming, movies, and dining under one roof. This project underscores the growing trend of destination-style entertainment complexes anchoring urban redevelopment.

articles.title: Round One Posts Double-Digit Sales Growth in Japan Entertainment Operations for June
articles.summary: Round One, a major FEC operator with significant U.S. presence, reported double-digit sales growth across its Japan entertainment locations in June. Strong performance data from Round One is a positive indicator for the broader amusement and FEC sector heading into the back half of the year. U.S. operators can look to Round One's model as a benchmark for diversified entertainment offerings driving revenue growth.

articles.title: Six Flags Appoints Mark Pauls as New Chief Operating Officer
articles.summary: Six Flags has named Mark Pauls as its new Chief Operating Officer, succeeding Tim Fisher who transitions to a Special Advisor role. Leadership changes at one of the largest regional theme park operators can signal shifts in operational strategy that ripple across the broader attractions industry. FEC operators and suppliers should monitor any strategic pivots that follow under new leadership.

articles.title: Market Research Breaks Down Major Segments Shaping the Family and Indoor Entertainment Center Industry
articles.summary: A new market analysis identifies and examines the key segments driving growth in the family and indoor entertainment center sector. Understanding segment performance — from trampoline parks to arcade-redemption venues — helps operators and investors allocate resources effectively. This type of industry-wide research is essential reading for anyone planning new locations or product investments.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Launches in London as Part of £20M UK Expansion
articles.summary: Indoor golf concept Five Iron Golf has opened its first London location as part of an ambitious £20 million UK expansion plan. The move signals strong international appetite for tech-enhanced indoor sports entertainment beyond the U.S. market. FEC operators considering golf simulator or social sports concepts can look to Five Iron's rapid growth as a proof-of-concept for the format.

articles.title: LEVEL 99 Expanding to Connecticut with First West Hartford Location
articles.summary: LEVEL 99, the immersive physical gaming and challenge venue concept, is opening its first Connecticut location in West Hartford. The brand's continued geographic expansion validates strong consumer demand for immersive, activity-based entertainment experiences. FEC operators should take note of LEVEL 99's format as a competitive benchmark in the immersive entertainment space.

articles.title: The Breakers Resort Reveals Renderings for Planned Family Entertainment Center
articles.summary: Luxury resort The Breakers in Palm Beach has unveiled renderings for a planned Family Entertainment Center, reflecting a growing trend of upscale hospitality properties integrating FEC amenities. Adding an FEC to a high-end resort environment targets affluent families and expands revenue opportunities beyond traditional hotel offerings. Operators and designers can study this project as an example of FEC integration into premium hospitality.

articles.title: New Plans Unveiled for West Des Moines Waterpark and Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: Developers have released updated plans for a large-scale waterpark and entertainment complex in West Des Moines, pointing to continued investment in multi-attraction leisure destinations in mid-sized markets. Projects like this often include FEC components such as arcades, mini golf, and indoor play. Operators and suppliers in the Midwest should track this development for potential partnership and competitive landscape implications.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley & Eats Opens as Delaware's Largest Indoor Entertainment Destination in Newark
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley & Eats has opened a new location in Newark, Delaware, positioning itself as the state's largest indoor entertainment venue and bringing bowling, dining, and arcade experiences to the market. The venue's expansion from beach markets to an inland urban location demonstrates the viability of multi-concept FEC formats in varied demographics. FEC operators can study Lefty's approach to combining food and beverage with entertainment as a revenue diversification model.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Opens New Location in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new venue in Carrollton, Georgia, adding another FEC option to a smaller regional market. New FEC openings in non-major metros highlight the continued growth of the sector beyond large urban centers. Industry suppliers and franchisors should note the opportunity in secondary markets as operators seek less competitive locations.

articles.title: The Rush Funplex Expands to Mesa, Arizona
articles.summary: The Rush Funplex, a multi-attraction indoor entertainment brand, is expanding to Mesa, Arizona, continuing its growth in the Sun Belt market. Trampoline and action park concepts like The Rush continue to find strong demand in fast-growing suburban areas. This expansion is relevant for operators and real estate partners tracking FEC growth patterns in the Southwest.

articles.title: Former Johnson County Craft Store Converting to Family Action Park
articles.summary: A former craft store space in Johnson County, Kansas is being redeveloped into a family action park, illustrating the trend of repurposing retail real estate for FEC use. This type of adaptive reuse is increasingly common as vacant big-box and specialty retail spaces become cost-effective FEC venues. Operators and developers looking for real estate opportunities should follow this trend closely.

articles.title: Parky's FEC at Moorestown Mall Back on Track After Lawsuit Resolved
articles.summary: Parky's Family Entertainment Center at Moorestown Mall in New Jersey is moving forward with its development after a lawsuit that had stalled the project was settled. Mall-based FEC projects face unique legal and logistical hurdles, making this resolution a notable case study for operators pursuing similar locations. The project's restart also reflects continued investor confidence in mall-anchored entertainment concepts.

articles.title: How to Build a Profitable Laser Tag Business: Lessons from a Mobile Operator
articles.summary: A detailed operational guide explores how laser tag entrepreneurs can build sustainable businesses, featuring insights from James Goldstein of Laser Tag on the Go in Connecticut, who chose a fully mobile model over a fixed venue. The article covers business model selection, systems, and customer experience as key differentiators for profitability. FEC operators considering laser tag as an attraction or standalone concept will find actionable takeaways here.

articles.title: Massanutten Resort Debuts Virginia's First Alpine Coaster Attraction
articles.summary: Massanutten Resort in Virginia's Shenandoah Valley has opened Mountain Mayhem, the state's first alpine coaster, as part of its Family Adventure Park. Alpine coasters are a growing FEC-adjacent attraction that can extend seasonal appeal and draw repeat visitors to resort and outdoor entertainment destinations. Operators looking to add outdoor thrill attractions should note the strong branding and regional milestone marketing angle Massanutten is leveraging.

articles.title: Hasbro Expands into Experiential Entertainment with The Play District Project
articles.summary: Hasbro is expanding into experiential entertainment with The Play District, a new initiative designed to bring its IP to life in physical, interactive retail and entertainment environments. Major toy and game IP entering the FEC and experiential space represents both a competitive and partnership opportunity for existing FEC operators. Operators running arcade, play, and family entertainment venues should monitor Hasbro's IP licensing strategy for potential collaboration opportunities.

articles.title: Alterface and Fantawild Partner to Develop New Interactive Attractions
articles.summary: Interactive attractions technology leader Alterface has formed a partnership with Chinese theme park giant Fantawild to co-develop unique interactive attraction experiences. Alterface's technology is widely used in shooting and interactive dark rides, making this partnership relevant for FEC operators interested in cutting-edge interactive attraction systems. The collaboration could yield new products and technologies that eventually reach the broader FEC market.

articles.title: Heat Wave Drives Families to Indoor Entertainment Venues, Boosting FEC Business in Greensboro
articles.summary: Greensboro, North Carolina businesses including indoor entertainment venues are reporting a noticeable increase in customer traffic as families seek air-conditioned alternatives during a summer heat wave. This seasonal pattern underscores the competitive advantage indoor FECs hold over outdoor attractions during extreme weather. Operators should leverage heat wave marketing strategies to maximize this natural traffic driver.

articles.title: Triad Region Indoor Businesses See Customer Surge During Heatwave
articles.summary: Indoor entertainment and amusement businesses in North Carolina's Triad region are experiencing higher foot traffic as summer heat pushes families indoors. The trend reinforces the strategic value of indoor FEC positioning as a weather-proof entertainment alternative. This real-world data point supports investment in indoor attractions as a hedge against outdoor competition.

articles.title: Fiesta Village FEC in Colton, California Draws Final Crowds Before Permanent Closure
articles.summary: Fiesta Village, a long-running family entertainment center in Colton, California, has permanently closed, drawing nostalgic final crowds ahead of shuttering. The closure of an established FEC highlights the ongoing challenges facing older, single-location operators competing against modern multi-concept venues. Industry observers should note the factors behind this closure as a cautionary tale for operators who have not updated their offerings.

articles.title: Magic Mountain Fun Center East in Columbus Permanently Closes
articles.summary: Magic Mountain Fun Center East in Columbus, Ohio has permanently closed, adding to a pattern of legacy FEC closures in competitive markets. The shuttering follows a trend of older, smaller FECs struggling to compete against newer, larger entertainment complexes. Operators in similar positions should evaluate reinvestment in their facilities to remain competitive.

articles.title: FEC Spotlight: FatCats and Blazer Tag Featured in Fun Across America Series
articles.summary: RePlay Magazine's Fun Across America series spotlights FatCats and Blazer Tag as examples of thriving FEC operations blending multiple entertainment formats. These profiles offer operational and marketing insights from established multi-concept FEC brands. Operators looking for inspiration in diversifying their attraction mix can draw lessons from both venues.

articles.title: Zamania by Leo & Loona Opens New Family Entertainment Venue at Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai
articles.summary: Zamania by Leo & Loona has opened a new family entertainment center at Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai, expanding the brand's footprint in the Middle East's growing FEC market. Mall-based FECs in the GCC region continue to attract investment as developers seek family-friendly anchor tenants for large retail destinations. International FEC operators and suppliers should note the expanding opportunity in Middle Eastern markets.

articles.title: Sky Zone Trampoline Park Brings New Family Entertainment and Jobs to Local Market
articles.summary: A new Sky Zone Trampoline Park location is opening, bringing family entertainment options and local employment to its community. Sky Zone's continued expansion reinforces trampoline parks as one of the most consistently growing FEC formats. Franchise operators and real estate investors should note Sky Zone's site selection criteria as a benchmark for trampoline park viability.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure Teases Mysterious 'Project Purple' New Attraction Announcement
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure has released a cryptic teaser video for Project Purple, with a full announcement expected later in July. Attraction reveals from major regional park operators generate significant consumer buzz and can influence competitive positioning for nearby FECs. Operators in the greater New York-New Jersey market should monitor this announcement for potential impact on their guest traffic.

articles.title: PortAventura World and Vekoma Create Mixed Reality Experience at Roman Amphitheater
articles.summary: PortAventura World and ride manufacturer Vekoma have collaborated on a mixed reality attraction overlaid on a historic Roman amphitheater, requiring no permanent physical changes to the site. This innovative use of mixed reality technology to enhance existing spaces is directly applicable to FEC operators looking to add tech-driven experiences without major capital investment. The project demonstrates how XR technology can create compelling new attractions within existing infrastructure.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Officially Opens in Frisco, Texas
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort has officially opened in Frisco, Texas, featuring seven themed areas built around beloved children's IP after three years of development. The resort's opening represents a major new competitor in the Texas family entertainment market and signals Universal's commitment to expanding beyond its Florida and California anchors. FEC operators in the Dallas-Fort Worth metro should assess how this major new arrival may affect their guest demographics and visit frequency.

articles.title: Fever Partners with London & Partners to Support Experience Economy Growth in London
articles.summary: Global live entertainment platform Fever has partnered with London & Partners to help experience creators launch, expand, and reach audiences in London, as part of the city's broader growth strategy. This partnership provides ticketing expertise, market insights, and venue connections to experience operators looking to enter or scale in the London market. FEC and immersive entertainment operators with international ambitions should explore what this platform partnership could offer for UK expansion.
